apply: Students will be able to apply the periodic trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity to predict the reactivity of s-block elements.
Students will be able to use the knowledge of s-block elements to predict the formation and properties of compounds.
Students will be able to apply the concept of electron gain enthalpy to explain the reactivity of s-block elements.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the trends in the chemical properties of s-block elements and explain their variations.
Students will be able to compare the properties of alkali metals and alkaline earth metals.
Students will be able to analyze the role of s-block elements in the formation of various compounds and their applications.
remember: Students will be able to recall the electronic configuration and general characteristics of s-block elements.
Students will be able to identify and write the general electronic configuration of alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, and hydrogen.
Students will be able to recognize the trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity in s-block elements.
understand: Students will be able to explain the periodic trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity in s-block elements.
Students will be able to describe the reactivity of s-block elements and their tendency to lose electrons.
Students will be able to understand the formation of compounds and their properties in s-block elements.

ncert_objectives: Explain the general trends in the properties of s-block elements.
Discuss the characteristics of alkali metals and alkaline earth metals.
Describe the formation of compounds and their properties in s-block elements.
board_exam_objectives: Identify the general electronic configuration of alkali metals and alkaline earth metals.
Explain the trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity in s-block elements.
Describe the reactivity of s-block elements and their tendency to lose electrons.
